name: llada2-text-diffusion
description: Accelerate text generation with hybrid masking strategies. Draft-and-edit text synthesis via token editing. Speed up diffusion language models 2-3x. Balance generation quality and decoding speed. Implement Speedy Mode and Quality Mode switching.
paperTitle: LLaDA2.1: Speeding Up Text Diffusion via Token Editing
content:
# LLaDA2.1: Speeding Up Text Diffusion via Token Editing

## Core Insight

Text diffusion models face a critical trade-off: Mask-to-Token (M2T) drafting is fast but can produce suboptimal results, while extensive refinement improves quality but increases latency. LLaDA2.1 solves this through a joint M2T + Token-to-Token (T2T) editing framework with configurable threshold-decoding. This enables two complementary modes: Speedy Mode (S-Mode) with aggressive drafting corrected via T2T, and Quality Mode (Q-Mode) with conservative drafting. The approach, trained via RL for discrete diffusion, achieves 892 TPS on HumanEval+ (100B model) with strong benchmark performance.

## Implementation

```python
import numpy as np
from typing import List, Tuple, Optional, Dict
from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum

class DecodingMode(Enum):
    SPEEDY = "speedy"      # S-Mode: aggressive drafting + heavy editing
    QUALITY = "quality"    # Q-Mode: conservative drafting + light editing
    BALANCED = "balanced"  # Mid-threshold

@dataclass
class TokenEdits:
    """Track Token-to-Token edits during decoding."""
    original_tokens: List[int]
    edited_tokens: List[int]
    edit_positions: List[int]
    edit_count: int
    total_positions: int

class LLaDA2Generator:
    """Text generation via Mask-to-Token drafting + Token-to-Token editing."""

    def __init__(self, vocab_size: int = 50000, max_seq_len: int = 512):
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
        self.max_seq_len = max_seq_len
        self.m2t_threshold = 0.5  # Configurable
        self.t2t_threshold = 0.7

    def generate_with_editing(
        self,
        prompt: str,
        max_new_tokens: int = 100,
        mode: DecodingMode = DecodingMode.BALANCED
    ) -> Tuple[str, TokenEdits]:
        """Generate text using M2T drafting + T2T editing."""

        # Convert prompt to tokens (mock implementation)
        input_ids = self._encode(prompt)

        # Set thresholds based on mode
        if mode == DecodingMode.SPEEDY:
            m2t_thresh = 0.3   # Aggressive drafting
            t2t_thresh = 0.6   # Heavy editing
        elif mode == DecodingMode.QUALITY:
            m2t_thresh = 0.7   # Conservative drafting
            t2t_thresh = 0.8   # Light editing
        else:  # BALANCED
            m2t_thresh = 0.5
            t2t_thresh = 0.7

        # Phase 1: Mask-to-Token drafting
        draft_tokens = self._mask_to_token_draft(
            input_ids, max_new_tokens, threshold=m2t_thresh
        )

        # Phase 2: Token-to-Token editing refinement
        final_tokens, edits = self._token_to_token_edit(
            input_ids + draft_tokens, threshold=t2t_thresh
        )

        output_text = self._decode(final_tokens)
        return output_text, edits

    def _mask_to_token_draft(
        self,
        input_ids: List[int],
        max_new_tokens: int,
        threshold: float = 0.5
    ) -> List[int]:
        """
        Mask-to-Token (M2T) phase: Fill masked positions with high-confidence tokens.

        Standard diffusion decoder: iteratively fill [MASK] tokens with highest
        confidence predictions until convergence.
        """
        sequence = input_ids.copy()
        mask_token_id = 103  # [MASK] token ID

        # Create initial mask for new positions
        current_masks = [mask_token_id] * max_new_tokens
        sequence.extend(current_masks)

        iteration = 0
        max_iterations = 20

        while mask_token_id in sequence and iteration < max_iterations:
            # Get confidence scores for each masked position
            confidences = self._get_token_confidences(sequence)

            # Find positions with confidence > threshold
            mask_positions = [i for i, token in enumerate(sequence) if token == mask_token_id]

            # For each masked position, predict token with confidence score
            new_tokens = []
            for pos in mask_positions:
                conf_score = confidences[pos] if pos < len(confidences) else 0.0

                if conf_score > threshold:
                    # Fill with predicted token
                    predicted_token = self._predict_token_at_position(sequence, pos)
                    new_tokens.append((pos, predicted_token))
                    sequence[pos] = predicted_token

            if not new_tokens:
                break

            iteration += 1

        # Remove remaining masks, return only new tokens
        return [t for t in sequence[len(input_ids):] if t != mask_token_id]

    def _token_to_token_edit(
        self,
        sequence: List[int],
        threshold: float = 0.7,
        max_edits: int = 50
    ) -> Tuple[List[int], TokenEdits]:
        """
        Token-to-Token (T2T) phase: Refine drafted tokens by swapping suboptimal ones.

        For each already-placed token, evaluate if replacement would improve fidelity.
        This is the novel editing mechanism that improves quality without full redrafting.
        """
        edited_sequence = sequence.copy()
        edits_made = []

        # Iterate over tokens, identifying candidates for improvement
        for position in range(len(sequence)):
            if position < len(sequence) - 1:  # Don't edit prompt tokens
                current_token = edited_sequence[position]

                # Score current token at this position
                current_score = self._score_token_at_position(edited_sequence, position)

                # Find alternative tokens with higher scores
                alternative_candidates = self._get_alternative_tokens(
                    edited_sequence, position, top_k=5
                )

                for alt_token, alt_score in alternative_candidates:
                    if alt_score > current_score and alt_score > threshold:
                        # Perform edit
                        edited_sequence[position] = alt_token
                        edits_made.append((position, current_token, alt_token))
                        break

                if len(edits_made) >= max_edits:
                    break

        original_from_draft = sequence[len(sequence) - len([t for t in sequence]):]
        edits = TokenEdits(
            original_tokens=sequence,
            edited_tokens=edited_sequence,
            edit_positions=[e[0] for e in edits_made],
            edit_count=len(edits_made),
            total_positions=len(sequence)
        )

        return edited_sequence, edits

    def _get_token_confidences(self, sequence: List[int]) -> List[float]:
        """Get per-token confidence scores from model."""
        # Mock implementation: return random scores
        return np.random.uniform(0, 1, len(sequence)).tolist()

    def _predict_token_at_position(self, sequence: List[int], position: int) -> int:
        """Predict most likely token at position."""
        return np.random.randint(0, self.vocab_size)

    def _score_token_at_position(self, sequence: List[int], position: int) -> float:
        """Score how well current token fits at position."""
        return np.random.uniform(0.4, 0.95)

    def _get_alternative_tokens(
        self,
        sequence: List[int],
        position: int,
        top_k: int = 5
    ) -> List[Tuple[int, float]]:
        """Get top-k alternative tokens with scores for position."""
        candidates = [
            (np.random.randint(0, self.vocab_size), np.random.uniform(0.5, 1.0))
            for _ in range(top_k)
        ]
        return sorted(candidates, key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)

    def _encode(self, text: str) -> List[int]:
        """Mock: encode text to token IDs."""
        return [hash(c) % self.vocab_size for c in text.split()]

    def _decode(self, token_ids: List[int]) -> str:
        """Mock: decode token IDs to text."""
        return " ".join(f"tok_{t}" for t in token_ids[:10])

class PerformanceBenchmark:
    """Measure generation speed and quality."""

    def __init__(self, generator: LLaDA2Generator):
        self.generator = generator

    def benchmark_throughput(
        self,
        num_samples: int = 100,
        sequence_length: int = 100
    ) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Measure tokens-per-second for each mode."""
        results = {}

        for mode in [DecodingMode.SPEEDY, DecodingMode.QUALITY, DecodingMode.BALANCED]:
            import time
            start = time.time()

            for _ in range(num_samples):
                self.generator.generate_with_editing(
                    "def solve(",
                    max_new_tokens=sequence_length,
                    mode=mode
                )

            elapsed = time.time() - start
            tokens_generated = num_samples * sequence_length
            tps = tokens_generated / elapsed

            results[mode.value] = tps

        return results

    def benchmark_quality(self, eval_set: List[str]) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Evaluate output quality using task-specific metrics."""
        # Mock: return synthetic scores
        return {
            DecodingMode.SPEEDY.value: 0.72,
            DecodingMode.QUALITY.value: 0.88,
            DecodingMode.BALANCED.value: 0.82
        }
```

## Practical Guidance

**Mode Selection**: Use Speedy Mode (S-Mode) for latency-critical applications (interactive chat, real-time coding). Use Quality Mode (Q-Mode) for production workflows requiring high-quality outputs. Balanced mode is suitable for general-purpose use.

**Threshold Tuning**: Start with m2t_threshold=0.5 (50% confidence for drafting), t2t_threshold=0.7 (70% for editing). In Speedy Mode, lower m2t to 0.3 (aggressive drafting) and lower t2t to 0.6 (heavy editing). In Quality Mode, raise both to 0.7+ (conservative approach).

**Editing Budget**: Allocate 20-50 edits per sequence (position swaps that improve fidelity). Track edit_count to verify T2T is actually improving outputs. If edits exceed 40% of sequence length, quality may be degrading.

**Performance Expectations**: Speedy Mode should achieve 800+ TPS on long sequences (100B model). Quality Mode trades 20-30% latency for +10-15% accuracy improvement. Balanced mode splits the difference.

**RL Training**: If fine-tuning on custom domains, train via discrete diffusion RL with reward signal from held-out validation set. Use specialized gradient estimators (Gumbel-Softmax variants) for stable training.

## Reference

- LLaDA2.1 paper: [arXiv 2602.08676](https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08676)
- GitHub repository: [inclusionAI/LLaDA2.X](https://github.com/inclusionAI/LLaDA2.X)
- Models: LLaDA2.1-Mini (16B), LLaDA2.1-Flash (100B)
- Performance: 892 TPS HumanEval+, 801 TPS BigCodeBench, 663 TPS LiveCodeBench
